title: Update: Abigail battles Civil War for the #1 spot at the weekend box office
text: Abigail earned $1 million in Thursday previews, including previews from IMAX and large-format venues. The R-rated horror film was projected to compete closely with A24's Civil War for the #1 spot at the weekend box office. Both films targeted adult audiences and were expected to perform well in their respective categories. Civil War was coming off a record-setting $25.7 million opening weekend for A24, making it the distributor's strongest debut ever. Abigail's competitive Thursday preview numbers suggested it had potential to take the top spot despite Civil War's strong hold. However, Abigail ultimately came in second place with a $10.2 million opening, finishing behind Civil War's $11.1 million second weekend total. The close competition between these two adult-oriented films highlighted a crowded marketplace for R-rated content during the Easter weekend period.
